text:
BOTTOM LINE — Inflation priced, energy bid, growth cracked.
Today’s tape was a rotation trade, not a risk-on expansion, with oil +4.07% forcing leadership into XLE, XLV, and XLP while QQQ -0.85% and IWM -0.97% absorbed the damage. Positioning leaned defensive: SPY put-call 2.04, QQQ put-call 1.60, and QQQ skew +10.4% show protection demand, while VIX 18.0 stayed only 0.1 below its 20-day mean. The CPI print at 3.8% headline and 2.8% core kept the Fed at hold=100% for Jun 17, 2026, so the tape said “higher-for-longer” without triggering a full volatility break.

INDICES — VIX 18.0, flat. DIA +0.16%, SPY -0.15%, QQQ -0.85%, IWM -0.97%. Small < large by 0.82pp; USO +4.07% was the sharpest cross-asset move. SPY and QQQ are not below the 200-day MA in the data.

VOLUME — DIA 1.7x and QQQ-related XLK 1.5x drew the most attention, while XLE 0.9x, XLI 0.8x, and XLY 0.8x were being ignored. SPY volume ratio was not provided; QQQ vol_ratio was not provided, but VXN 24.1 and QQQ -0.85% signal distribution, not accumulation. IWM 1.0x with -0.97% and IJR 0.9x with -1.14% fit a low-conviction, lower-quality risk-off tape.

SECTORS — XLV Healthcare +1.96%, XLP Staples +1.28%, XLF Financials +0.78%, XLE Energy +0.70%, XLC Comms +0.24%, XLU Utilities +0.11%, XLRE Real Estate +0.02%, XLB Materials -0.23%, XLI Industrials -0.39%, XLY Discretionary -0.90%, XLK Technology -1.51%. XLV +1.96% and XLP +1.28% led; XLK -1.51% and XLY -0.90% lagged. XLV had the strongest industries read via PPH +1.48% on 3.1x volume, while XLE’s weakest read was XTN -0.3x volume on sector rotation.

THE 7s — Top 5: Medtech +2.13%, Health +1.65%, Defense +1.60%, Old Staples +1.41%, Energy +0.90%. Bottom 5: Mag -0.47%, Power -0.51%, Freight -0.73%, Industrial -0.78%, Housing -0.85%. Strength: Medtech via IHI +2.42%; weakness: Energy via the weakest ticker was not given, but the basket was broad and clean; Semi -3.81% had SOXX pressure, while Fab -2.49% stayed internally strong on 20d +11.92%.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is Jun 17, 2026, with CME hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%, and the stance tally is Hawk 6, Neutral 5, Dove 4. USD liquidity stress is 30/100, Normal; the most-stressed component is Reserve Scarcity at the 85th percentile, with TED spread 0.09% and ON-RRP $3.7B both benign.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 2.04 and QQQ PCR 1.60 show explicit protection demand, and SPY skew +8.1% plus QQQ skew +10.4% keep fear premium elevated. SPY ATM IV 12.6% sits below VIX 18.0 versus its 20-day 18.1, while QQQ ATM IV 20.1% with VXN 24.1 says Nasdaq hedging is still active. SPY GEX flip 746.0 is close to spot, so dealer hedging can still magnify a move.

CROSS-ASSET — USO +4.07% and OVX 75.7 reinforced the sector split into XLE +0.70% and XLK -1.51%. Gold GLD -0.40% against oil +4.07% is a classic inflation impulse, not a panic bid.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.2/100, neutral. Top movers: USO +4.07% in Cross-asset, LIT -2.35% in Industries, and QQQ -0.85% in Indices. 20d momentum: best sector XLK +18.43%, worst sector XLV -2.00%, best basket Buildout +43.81%, worst basket Semi +43.33%; CPER +2.67% on 2.2x volume was accumulation.

WATCH — QQQ -0.85%: PCR 1.60, skew +10.4%, VXN 24.1, and 1.5x XLK volume make this a momentum-break watch. SPY -0.15%: GEX flip 746.0 and PCR 2.04 keep dealer hedging in focus around spot. IWM -0.97%: small-cap underperformance versus DIA +0.16% and 1.0x volume argue for continued caution on breadth.
